title: Migrating from MyISAM to InnoDB
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. ALTER TABLE rewrites; offline if huge.
2. Per-table off-peak.
3. pt-online-schema-change for zero-downtime.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.
solution:
-- Use pt-online-schema-change
pt-online-schema-change --alter="ENGINE=InnoDB" --execute D=mydb,t=orders
what_breaks_naive_approach: ALTER on huge table locks for hours.

title: In-app feature flag adoption
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Flags are TEMPORARY; cleanup process.
2. Targeting per user/cohort.
3. SDK eval cached locally.
feature_flag_strategy: Itself

title: Webpack 4 → 5
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Polyfill changes (no auto-polyfill in v5).
2. Plugin compat varies.
3. Persistent cache big win.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.

title: Recovering from a botched migration
migration_strategy: Phase 3: Restore from backup or recompute
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. First, stop bleeding.
2. Quantify scope.
3. Restore from backup if possible; recompute from source data otherwise.
4. Post-mortem.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.

title: Replacing Backbone with React
migration_strategy: Phase 1: React mount points alongside Backbone
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Coexistence period; each renders its own DOM region.
2. Per-view migration.
feature_flag_strategy: Per-view
solution:
-- Replacing Backbone with React
-- Phase 1: add compatible structure without changing reads.
ALTER TABLE target_table ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS new_value TEXT;

-- Phase 2: backfill in bounded chunks with progress tracking.
UPDATE target_table
SET new_value = legacy_value
WHERE new_value IS NULL
  AND id IN (SELECT id FROM target_table WHERE new_value IS NULL ORDER BY id LIMIT 1000);

-- Phase 3: dual-read/dual-write in app code, compare counts, then cut over.
-- Phase 4: remove legacy path only after telemetry shows zero legacy reads for one full release window.

-- Rollback: keep legacy column/path until validation passes; disable new writes with a feature flag.

title: Pg_stat_statements rollout
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Requires restart of Postgres for shared_preload_libraries.
2. Schedule restart with replication failover.
3. Dashboard surfaces top-N slow.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.

title: OpenAPI generation from code
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Single source of truth.
2. Generated spec deployed alongside.
3. Documentation autoupdates.
rollback_procedure: Rollback by disabling the feature flag, routing traffic back to the previous path, replaying only idempotent jobs, and verifying error-rate and data-drift counters before cleanup.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.

title: Splitting React props with TypeScript
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Compound component pattern.
2. Slot-based.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.
telemetry_requirements: Emit rollout, error-rate, latency, adoption, rollback, and user-impact metrics with enough tags to compare old and new paths.
solution:
// before
<MegaCard title="x" subtitle="y" image="z" footer={...} />

// after
<Card>
  <Card.Header>x</Card.Header>
  <Card.Subtitle>y</Card.Subtitle>
  <Card.Image src="z"/>
  <Card.Footer>...</Card.Footer>
</Card>

title: Migrating timezone-naive timestamps
migration_strategy: Phase 1: Determine original TZ per row source
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. Audit data sources.
2. Per-source conversion.
3. Validate via sample.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.
solution:
-- Per source mapping
UPDATE events SET created_at_utc = created_at AT TIME ZONE source_tz;

title: Yarn → pnpm

title: Adopting connection pooling (PgBouncer)
migration_strategy: Phase 2: App configured to use pooler endpoint
reasoning:
Senior thinking:
1. transaction pooling mode = max throughput, no session features.
2. session pool = compat, less efficient.
3. Test prepared statements behavior.
feature_flag_strategy: Ship behind a reversible flag with owner, expiry date, cohort targeting, metrics, and an emergency disable path exercised before rollout.
solution:
# pgbouncer.ini
pool_mode = transaction
max_client_conn = 1000
default_pool_size = 25

# app DB_URL = postgres://app:x@pgbouncer:6432/db
